sections.paragraph:
ἐκκακέω 1 aor. ἐξεκάκησα (ἐκ + κακός; Vett. Val. 201, 15; Herm. Wr., Fgm. XXIII 46 [484, 3 Sc.]; Hesych.; Sym. Jer 18:12; TestJob 24:10; Philo, Conf. Lingu. 51 codd.) lose heart (w. διψυχέω.—Astrampsychus p. 31 Dec. 61, 3) Hm 9:8. V.l. for ἐγκακέω q.v.: Lk 18:1; 2 Cor 4:1, 16; Gal 6:9; Eph 3:13; 2 Th 3:13.—M-M. s.v. ἐνκακέω.

aor. aor. = aorist

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Hesych Hesych , V a.d.—List 5

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Astrampsychus Astrampsychus , before IV b.c.; later works associated with his name, date uncertain—List 5

Hm Hm = Mandates

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 303
